label: FreshBooks
description: Cloud accounting software for small businesses — manage invoices, expenses, and payments.

form.title: Connect FreshBooks
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your FreshBooks account via OAuth 2.0.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Connect via FreshBooks OAuth 2.0. Create a FreshBooks app to get a client ID and secret, then Anton opens a browser window for you to grant access. No password stored — revocable anytime from FreshBooks.
methods.how_to:
## Connect FreshBooks via OAuth 2.0

You'll need to register a FreshBooks developer app to get a client ID and secret.

### Steps

1. Go to [FreshBooks Developer Portal](https://my.freshbooks.com/#/developer) and sign in.
2. Click **Create an App**.
3. Fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and any required fields. For the **Redirect URI**, enter `http://localhost` (or the URI Anton instructs you to use during setup).
4. Click **Save**. Your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** are shown on the app detail page.
5. Copy both values and paste them into the fields below.
6. Click **Submit**. Anton will open your browser to FreshBooks' consent screen.
7. Sign in (if prompted) and click **Allow Access**.
8. Anton receives the authorization code, exchanges it for tokens, and stores them locally.

### Notes

- FreshBooks issues refresh tokens with long lifetimes, but if access is revoked you'll need to re-authorize.
- To revoke access, visit **FreshBooks → Settings → Connected Apps** and remove Anton.
- Scopes are managed by your app's settings on the FreshBooks developer portal.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Found on your app's detail page at my.freshbooks.com/#/developer.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with your client ID — shown once when you create or reset the app secret.
